summary: PR #2619 was a closed, unmerged split-out matching-improvement slice scoped to src/melee/cm, with the PR body listing src/melee/cm/camera.c. The loaded raw diff and bot report show no changes for the slice, but a review comment on camera.c flags a proposed replacement of HSD_ASSERTMSG calls with raw __assert calls using cm_803BCBD0/cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30 as a data-section-matching regression.

key_files.role: Only file listed in PR scope/files and the file targeted by the review comment; concrete loaded hunk concerns Camera_80029CF8 assert handling.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: Path src/melee/cm/camera.c, hunk in Camera_80029CF8

what_changed.text: The PR was presented as a directory/subsystem split from #2617 for src/melee/cm matching improvements.
The PR body reported the full unsplit source branch had matching improvements, but stated per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting would be authoritative for the slice.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes

what_changed.text: Loaded slice evidence contains no raw diff content and the decomp-dev bot reported 'No changes' for GALE01.
The review hunk shows an attempted replacement of two HSD_ASSERTMSG calls for fov_r/fov_l bounds with explicit if blocks calling __assert and address-style cm_* data symbols.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: hunk replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG at lines 0x508 and 0x509 with __assert(cm_803BCBD0, ..., cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30)

smart_moves.text: Splitting the broader #2617 work by directory/subsystem was intended to make matching improvements easier to review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so the matching improvements are easier to review

smart_moves.text: The PR body explicitly separated preliminary unsplit-branch stats from authoritative per-slice CI/decomp-dev reporting.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not replace assert/report macros with raw __assert calls and data-symbol operands merely to improve data-section matching; this can be considered a regression even if data percentage improves.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: reviewer says not to make changes with the intention of matching data sections and calls the highlighted change a regression
review_comments.md: hunk replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG with __assert and cm_* symbols

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching PRs, text-section progress should not be mixed with unrelated data-section symbol churn unless the PR evidence explains why the data change is required.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: change highlighted as made with intention of matching data sections

naming_conventions.convention: Keep readable macro-level assert message strings when represented by HSD_ASSERTMSG; do not convert them to address-style cm_803BCBD0/cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30 symbols without data ownership evidence.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: original HSD_ASSERTMSG strings 'fov_r<MTXDegToRad(90.0F)' and 'fov_l<MTXDegToRad(90.0F)' replaced by cm_* symbols

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoid using expanded raw __assert forms as a data matching tactic when the source-level HSD_ASSERTMSG macro expresses the operation.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: reviewer calls the __assert replacement a regression even if it improves data %

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Treat per-slice decomp-dev/checkdiff output as authoritative for split PR scoring; do not rely solely on full unsplit-branch stats.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes

review_feedback.feedback: Do not make changes with the intention of matching data sections; the highlighted HSD_ASSERTMSG-to-__assert change is a regression even if it improves data percentage.

follow_up_queries: Find PRs where HSD_ASSERTMSG or HSD_ASSERTREPORT was replaced by raw __assert for data-section matching.
Search camera.c history for cm_803BCBD0 cm_803BCC14 cm_803BCC30 and assert message ownership.
Find review comments mentioning 'matching data sections' and 'regression even if it improves the data %'.
Search split PRs from #2617 with decomp-dev 'No changes' reports.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: PR body, scope, preliminary report, local checks, listed file
review_comments.md: concrete reviewer feedback and diff hunk
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot 'No changes' and review state CHANGES_REQUESTED

merged_at is null and state is CLOSED, so no merge or accepted-code status is inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Raw __assert expansion for data-section matching can be a regression
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2619, a reviewer rejected replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG calls in src/melee/cm/camera.c with explicit __assert calls and cm_* data symbols, stating that changes intended to match data sections are not acceptable and that the highlighted change was a regression even if it improved data percentage.

source_update_candidates.title: Cross-reference assert macro cleanup with data-section matching regressions
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking an example: replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG with raw __assert plus generated/address-style data symbols to chase data-section percentage is a regression unless explicit source/data ownership evidence justifies it.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: reviewer says the highlighted data-section-matching change is a regression
review_comments.md: hunk shows HSD_ASSERTMSG replaced by __assert(cm_803BCBD0, ..., cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30)
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover this in general terms; the PR provides a concrete review example that may be useful as an illustrative source-owned standard note.

Do not infer that camera.c changes were merged or accepted; PR is closed with merged_at null.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit comment against data-section-matching changes and the CHANGES_REQUESTED review state.
